highlights.text: I wrote last Monday July 21st at 4pm (when we were 6340s): “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “The bull case though is that ES can fill it out the upside and test 6360, 6366, then 6371-74.

Rallies like this don’t come for free though and as I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “After a move like this, ES often needs to digest the move…My general lean if I had to give one though is that ES can cooloff now and do some backtesting and work on structure.” This is what we spent Monday and Tuesday doing, and ES began building a flag structure with support generally down at 6400, and resistance being in the mid-6430s.
I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is always defer to the trend.
My lean is any traps under are bought, then we head up to 6415, 6428, 6436”.
This gives us a chance to long and as readers know, I do that via one trade: The Failed Breakdown.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the difference between my core setup (The Failed Breakdown) and its more advanced sibling (the Level Reclaim).
For those who have mastered the classical Failed Breakdown, level reclaims are a useful tool for the toolkit and offer more entries.

post_title: The FOMC Dip Was Bought. Will It Stick Though? July 31 Plan
